assertions.expectation: Proposes a phased approach: control plane first, then CPU pools, then T4 dev, then inference (A100), then training (H100) last
assertions.reason: Plan clearly follows this exact sequence: Phase 1 (T4 dev), Phase 2 (CPU services), Phase 3 (A100 inference), Phase 4 (H100 training), with control plane upgrades mentioned first in each phase

assertions.expectation: Control plane can be upgraded independently without affecting running workloads
assertions.reason: Multiple examples show control plane upgrades with --master flag executed independently before node upgrades, such as 'gcloud container clusters upgrade dev-cluster --master --cluster-version=1.32.0-gke.1200'

assertions.expectation: CPU service pools upgraded first as lowest risk — use standard surge settings (maxSurge=2-3)
assertions.reason: CPU services are upgraded in Phase 2, not first (T4 dev is Phase 1), and uses maxSurge=20/maxUnavailable=10, not the expected standard 2-3 range

assertions.expectation: T4 dev pool upgraded next — dev workloads are tolerant of interruption
assertions.reason: T4 dev pool is actually upgraded first (Phase 1), not next after CPU pools, though the reasoning about dev workload tolerance is implicit

assertions.expectation: A100 inference pool upgraded with careful surge settings (maxSurge=1) and PDBs to maintain serving capacity
assertions.reason: Uses maxSurge=10/maxUnavailable=5, not the careful maxSurge=1 setting expected, and while traffic management is addressed, specific PDB configurations are not mentioned

assertions.expectation: H100 training pool upgraded last, during a scheduled gap between training runs — with cordon-and-wait or maxSurge=0/maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: H100 training is upgraded last but uses maxSurge=5/maxUnavailable=0, not the expected maxSurge=0/maxUnavailable=1, and doesn't explicitly mention scheduling during gaps between training runs

assertions.expectation: Notes the 20-node parallelism limit — at 5,000 nodes, the full upgrade will take weeks and must span multiple maintenance windows
assertions.reason: The 20-node parallelism limit is not mentioned anywhere in the response, though the plan does acknowledge the upgrade will take weeks (Phases 1-6 spanning 5-6 weeks)

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver compatibility testing in staging before each GPU pool upgrade
assertions.reason: GPU driver compatibility testing is not mentioned in the response for any of the GPU pools (T4, A100, H100)

assertions.expectation: Addresses GPUDirect/RDMA networking validation after upgrading the H100 pool
assertions.reason: GPUDirect/RDMA networking validation is not mentioned in the H100 training pool upgrade section or anywhere else in the response
